title: [Bug]: TLS context is not created when only NATS seed URLs use TLS
summary: The CLI creates a TLS context when the primary URL uses TLS, but not when TLS is present only in the configured seed URL list.
observed: A multi-server configuration using TLS seed URLs can be passed to the NATS client without a TLS context unless a certificate option is also configured.
expected: Any configured TLS NATS server URL must cause the CLI to build and pass an SSL context.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that configures only a TLS seed URL and inspects the NATS connection options.
impact: TLS seed-list deployments can fail or use incomplete TLS setup despite having valid TLS server URLs.
delivery_semantics: No ACK behavior changes. The fix affects connection setup before JetStream processing begins.
security: The fix ensures TLS transport intent is honored for all configured NATS server entries.
acceptance: A TLS context is created when any configured NATS seed URL uses TLS.
Primary TLS URL behavior continues to work.
Certificate and local CA options continue to be honored.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, NATS connection option tests, CLI tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update NATS TLS documentation if seed-list TLS behavior is clarified.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
